title: Crispy Peppery Norwegian Rye Flatbread
description: A juicy, peppery flatbread with a crisp rye crust, blanched red pepper toppings, and a lowsodium twist for an adventurous Norwegian snack.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together rye flour, instant yeast, and low‑sodium salt blend.
Add warm water and olive oil, stirring until a sticky dough forms.
Cover the dough and let it rest for 10 minutes to allow the yeast to activate.
Preheat oven to 220 °C (430 °F) and place a baking stone or steel inside.
Shape the dough into a 25 cm (10 in) round flatbread, brushing the surface lightly with olive oil.
Bring the water for blanch to a boil, then add the sliced bell pepper and blanch for 30 seconds; remove with tongs and set aside to cool.
After the dough has rested an additional 20 minutes, slide it onto the preheated stone and bake for 12–14 minutes until the crust is golden and crisp.
Remove the flatbread, top with blanched pepper slices, a sprinkle of fresh thyme, smoked paprika, and a light dusting of black pepper.
Slice the flatbread, serve immediately hot, and enjoy the peppery, juicy flavors.
